---
title: "QMD_class_1_Assignment"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

---
title: "TV Show Viewership Report: Game of Thrones"
author: "Ramik Sharma"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Introduction

*Game of Thrones* is an American fantasy drama television series created by David Benioff and D. B. Weiss for HBO. It is an adaptation of *A Song of Ice and Fire*, a series of fantasy novels by George R. R. Martin. The show premiered in 2011 and became a global cultural phenomenon, known for its complex characters, political intrigue, and unpredictable plot twists.

![Game of Thrones Title Card](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/d/d8/Game_of_Thrones_title_card.jpg)
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Create the dataset based on Wikipedia's average US viewership per season (in millions)
got_data <- data.frame(
  Season = 1:8,
  Viewers_Millions = c(2.52, 3.80, 4.97, 6.84, 6.88, 7.69, 10.26, 11.99)
)

# Calculate season-to-season changes
got_data <- got_data %>%
  mutate(Viewers_Change = Viewers_Millions - lag(Viewers_Millions))

# Extract specific numbers for inline references
s1_viewers <- got_data$Viewers_Millions[1]
s8_viewers <- got_data$Viewers_Millions[8]
change_s7_s8 <- round(got_data$Viewers_Millions[8] - got_data$Viewers_Millions[7], 2)
